title: Inside Atlassian’s Forge Billing Architecture for Distributed Usage Tracking at Scale
gist: Atlassian details its Forge billing platform, which turns distributed usage signals (function invocations, storage, telemetry) into accurate financial records via a Kafka-based ingestion layer and a Usage Tracking Service that validates, attributes, deduplicates, and aggregates events for usage-based pricing.

themes.theme: Usage-based billing architecture for a serverless platform

insights.claim: Atlassian's Forge serverless extensibility platform (used to build apps for Jira and Confluence) evolved from a simple extension model into a usage-driven ecosystem where billing depends on fine-grained signals such as function invocations, storage consumption, and operational telemetry.
insights.speaker: Leela Kumili
insights.so_what: Confirms the platform shift toward consumption pricing for serverless app ecosystems — billing infrastructure becomes a competitive requirement, not an afterthought.
insights.locator: The platform emerged as Forge evolved

insights.claim: Atlassian's Usage Tracking Service (UTS) acts as the coordination layer that validates, normalizes, enriches, deduplicates, and orders incoming usage events, ensuring each is attributed to the correct entitlement or subscription context before persistence and downstream billing.
insights.speaker: Leela Kumili
insights.so_what: Attribution correctness is the hard part of usage billing; a reference pattern for any SaaS moving to consumption pricing.
insights.quote: UTS (Usage Tracking Service), which acts as the nervous system for Forge Billing.

insights.claim: Forge billing uses idempotent event design and time-based/windowed aggregation so duplicate deliveries do not cause double-counting and late-arriving events are still incorporated, achieving financial correctness despite out-of-order or retried events over a Kafka-based streaming layer that decouples producers from consumers.
insights.speaker: Leela Kumili
insights.so_what: Idempotency plus windowed aggregation is the canonical recipe for billing-grade exactly-once accounting in distributed systems — reusable design guidance.
insights.quote: ensuring duplicate deliveries do not cause double counting while late-arriving events are incorporated through windowed processing

insights.claim: Forge stores usage data in layered systems — immutable long-term storage for auditability and a low-latency analytical layer for dashboards and APIs — mapping usage to developer spaces for per-app tracking and consolidated billing with traceability guarantees.
insights.speaker: Leela Kumili
insights.so_what: Dual-tier storage (immutable audit + fast analytics) is a pattern for combining financial auditability with near-real-time developer visibility.
insights.locator: Data is stored in layered systems
